title: dianthus campestris subsp. roseoluteus
intro: Dianthus campestris subsp. roseoluteus is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, known for its fragrant, pink or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, valued for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Dianthus campestris subsp. roseoluteus include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical ointments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
